evidence_refs.quote: higher crude oil prices raised our distribution fuel costs
crude oil price increases pushed jet fuel and diesel costs higher
parcel delivery volumes shifted between ground and air; our delivery mix moved toward ground freight
courier delivery mix shifted toward next-day parcel services
delivery as a share of restaurant orders grew; the delivery channel mix shifted from dine-in
same store sales grew 3.1% in the quarter

skips.why: Evidence ('alpha widget adoption drove the quarter') names a real cause — widget adoption — but the name does not carry that causal noun. 'unique' is a vague descriptor banned by R7 and fails R10 (not from source or catalog). No coined clean form (e.g. an adoption-metric name) exists in this catalog to rewrite to, and inventing one is forbidden, so skip.

Evidence ('beta segment pricing power held') names a real cause — segment pricing power — but 'beta_unique' does not name it. 'unique' is a vague filler word not present in the source (R7 vague descriptor, R10 noun-not-from-source). No coined pricing-power name exists in the catalog to rewrite to, so skip rather than admit a rule-breaking name.
